description: A wide daytime photograph of the facade of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building in Washington, D.C. under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds. The shot itself is a candid architectural exterior, but its dominant content is two enormous designed propaganda banners hung on the building. Flanking a large vertical American flag in the center, the left banner is a navy-blue portrait banner of President Trump with a small 'AMERICA 250' anniversary mark and large white serif text reading 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'; the right banner is a maroon/sepia-toned portrait of former President Theodore Roosevelt with the matching 'AMERICA 250' mark and 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' text. Tiny figures of people are visible on the roofline. The building's engraved name 'FRANCES PERKINS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING' is faintly visible above the entrance. The designed banners pair the current president with a historical Republican icon to project legitimacy and continuity behind the 'American Workers First' slogan as part of the 250th-anniversary patriotic branding. Propaganda technique: monumental display, association with a revered historical figure, and patriotic spectacle. Target audience is the general public and the administration's labor/nationalist base. (Marked designed because the banners are agency-produced propaganda graphics, though the file is a photograph of them.)
notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST. AMERICA 250. (Trump banner, left; Theodore Roosevelt banner, right.)
